person.name: William de Skipwith
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Sir William de Skipwith, knt, was lord of Skipwith in 1259. 

«b»Marraige & Children«/b»
Sir William de Skipwith married Alice, daughter of John and sister and heir to Sir William Thorpe, feudal lord of Kettleby Thorpe and Bigby, Lincolnshire and had:

1.) Anne, m Sir John d'Isney, of Norton Disney, Lincs;

2.) Catherine, m Sir Robert Constable, of Flamborough, Yorks, MP Yorks

3.) Sir John de Skipwith

sourceList.title: William Skipwith & Thorpe in Family Group Records Collection, Archives Section, 1942-1969; https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/3:1:3QS7-L97M-T8GJ?cc=2060211&wc=WWJR-JWL%3A352088301%2C353563801

zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Skipwith於大約1204年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾時，他的父親Reginald de Skipwith36歲，母親Isabel Bell Aqua32歲。他於1225年之前與Alice Thorpe在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1258年之後在英國越可爾斯吉維茲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Skipwith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: History: The main Skipwith family held the manor of Skipwith in England in the early Middle Ages, and direct descendants can be traced to the present day. In the 13th century they moved from Yorkshire to Lincolnshire, where their principal seat was at southern Ormsby. In the early 17th century there was further migration, to Leicestershire, Warwickshire, and across the Atlantic to VA. Other bearers of the name seem to have been tenants of Lincolnshire manors held by the Skipworth family, and to have taken the surname of their overlords.

variants.name: Skipworth
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): habitational name from Skipwith (East Yorkshire), from Old Northumbrian scīp (Old English scēap ‘sheep’), + Old English wīc ‘dwelling, specialized farm’, later replaced by Old Norse vithr ‘wood’; initial Sk-, instead of Sh-, is also due to Scandinavian influence.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Skipwith was born about 1204, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Reginald de Skipwith, was 36 and his mother, Isabel Bell Aqua, was 32. He married Alice Thorpe before 1225, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died after 1258, in Skipwith, Yorkshire, England.

Il a épousé Alice Thorpe avant 1225, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé après 1258, à Skipwith, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: William de Skipwith(이)가 대략 1204년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Reginald de Skipwith(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Bell Aqua(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1225년 이전 영국에서 Alice Thorpe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1258년 이후 영국 요르크실르 스키p위스에서 사망했다.
